description: Adverse drug effect symptom dictionary for prescribing cascade detection
disclaimer: For decision support only. Symptom matching is keyword-based and does not replace clinical assessment.

symptoms.cascade_relevance: Opioid-induced, CCB-induced, anticholinergic burden — may trigger laxative prescribing
Anticholinergic burden — may trigger prescribing of saliva substitutes or mouth-care agents
Antihypertensive over-treatment, orthostatic hypotension, vestibular ADEs, CCB over-dose
Sedation, orthostatic hypotension, anticholinergic burden, muscle relaxants
CNS depressants, opioids, benzodiazepines, first-generation antihistamines
Anticholinergic drugs, opioid-induced — may trigger prescribing of alpha-blockers or catheters
NSAID-induced, opioid-induced, ARV-induced — may trigger antiemetic prescribing
CCB-induced oedema — may trigger diuretic prescribing (cascade CC004)
Anticholinergic burden, opioids, benzodiazepines — may trigger antipsychotic prescribing
Efavirenz CNS effects (VIH004), stimulant drugs — may trigger hypnotic prescribing
